prompts: The challenge I am having with this is the workspace directories need to be available for me to choose.  I The challenge I'm having with this is I don't really know what the workspace directories are. This thing starts from the backend or the frontend or something like that, and I'm not entirely sure what is the list of projects that are available. I need, in some way, to identify what the workspaces are and the ability to pick and choose what those projects are so that I can operate in those projects.

The challenge I have with this application is it doesn't really fix the workspace route and the project, and it goes and creates artifacts all over the place. It is supposed to create work trees within the project so that it's easy, right? Now, maybe within workspaces, the hierarchy is workspace/project, and within that there'll be multiple work trees with multiple branches. I'm assuming that there will be some randomness in creating the work tree branches so that there are no duplicates there.

Essentially, all these features, when we are trying to implement them, need to be implemented in the work trees, and then the merge will actually happen right after that. Currently, it tries to create things all over the place, and it's very difficult to understand what it is doing. That's one of the usability things that are out there, and we need to fix that so that it is always pinned to a given project and is executing within the context of a project. Of course, you can switch the context and change it somewhere else, but it's very important that it stays laser focused for a given project.

Also, as you can imagine, in a given workspace there are different projects. Multiple people are working on a given project at any point in time. You should always be able to pull from remote for that particular branch, emerging into, and ensure that there are not many real conflicts when the merging actually happens. Those are some of the basic things that we need to fix in this one. Currently, it is kind of annoying that it is doing something on its own and there is no real control of what needs to happen here.
